name: Maya the Freelance Brand Designer
summary: Maya is a solo freelance brand designer who treats time like currency—every 10 minutes saved per project goes straight to her bottom line. She browses templates obsessively to kickstart client work, builds meticulous brand kits to enforce visual consistency, and needs a client-share experience that doesn't expose her tooling or undercut her professional image. White-labeling isn't a nice-to-have; it's how she justifies her rates.

location_signals: likely freelance hub city, US or EU, based on design_services industry
demographics.education_level: Bachelor's in Graphic Design, Visual Communication, or related field

goals: Save at least 10 minutes per project through templating and reusable brand kit components
Present deliverables to clients through a white-labeled share view that reinforces her own brand, not the tool's
Build and maintain a living brand kit for each client that enforces color, type, and asset consistency
Streamline the client feedback loop via comment threading so revision cycles don't drag out over email
Export production-ready assets quickly in the correct formats without extra manual steps
pains: Client share view exposes the tool's branding, which undermines her professional positioning and makes clients question why they're paying her rates
Template browsing sessions are long (20-35 min) but often end without finding a close enough starting point, forcing from-scratch work
Asset export requires too many steps or produces files that need post-processing before delivery
Comment threading notifications are scattered, making it hard to track which revision round a comment belongs to
No way to bundle brand assets, colors, and typography into a portable kit that travels cleanly across projects
motivations: Time is money—she bills hourly and any friction in her workflow directly reduces her effective hourly rate
Professional reputation depends on delivering a polished, seamless client experience end-to-end, not just the design files
Winning repeat business and referrals by making the review process feel effortless for non-designer clients
Building a scalable solo practice where systems do the administrative work so she can focus on creative output
objections: If the client share view shows your logo, I can't use it for client-facing deliverables—that's a dealbreaker
The template library isn't deep enough in brand identity categories to save me meaningful time on real projects
Monthly subscription cost needs to be offset by real time savings—I'm tracking this against my hourly rate
decision_triggers: White-label client share view is available at her plan tier
Template library has 50+ brand identity and marketing templates that are actually close to final quality
Time savings per project are measurable and exceed the subscription cost
Comment threading keeps revision history organized per deliverable, not per conversation
sample_quotes: I bill clients hourly so anything that saves me 10 minutes per project is worth real money. Can your client share view be white-labeled?
If the client sees your logo on the review link, they're going to ask me what tool I'm using and whether they should just use it themselves.
I don't want a template that's close—I want one I can use in 20 minutes. Otherwise I'm faster starting from scratch.
A brand kit that actually enforces rules across projects would save me the 'wait, that's not the right blue' conversation every single time.

journey_stages.mindset: Calculating whether the tool will pay for itself within the first 2-3 client projects
key_actions: template_browsing to assess template quality depth

journey_stages.mindset: Hands-on builder—setting up her workflow infrastructure before bringing in a real client
Routine user—measuring time saved per project and watching for friction that erodes the value prop
key_actions: Iterating on brand kit per client

source_evidence.claim: Freelance Brand Designer, company size 1, design_services industry
Saves time per project is directly tied to hourly billing income
Builds and maintains brand kits for clients
Uses comment threading to manage client feedback
Exports production-ready assets as part of delivery workflow
Client share view not white-labeled undermines professional positioning
Long template browsing sessions without finding ideal starting point
Asset export requires extra steps before delivery
Comment threading notifications are scattered across revision rounds
No portable brand kit that travels cleanly across projects
Time is money—billing hourly makes workflow friction directly costly
Professional reputation depends on polished client experience
Wins repeat business through effortless review process
Building scalable solo practice through systems
White-label absence is a dealbreaker for client-facing use
Template library depth is insufficient for brand identity work
Subscription cost must justify time savings vs. hourly rate
